info.title: Arcopay API PSD2 ENG
info.description: API standardization for a real-time connection to bank accounts.

get.description: Get a complete list of supported banks, as well as the names of the fields needed to draw a login form similar to the bank's original.

parameters.name: Accept-version
parameters.description: API version requested in format: v.numberOfVersion

parameters.name: countryCode
parameters.description: Country code, ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 format (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_3166-1_alpha-2). For example ES for Spain. If no code is provided, the entire list will be returned.

parameters.name: showSandbox
parameters.description: If no value is provided, sandbox service will not be informed. 1 to show only the sandbox service.

parameters.name: showExtendedInformation
parameters.description: If no value is provided, no additional parameters will be reported. 1 to display additional parameters of the call.

200.description: Array with the list of supported banks and the necessary information to draw your login form

post.summary: It manages the consent retrieval by the end user.
post.description:
The process of Obtaining consent for both Bank Aggregation and Payment Initiation is carried out in two phases:
1- First call to this method, generates a follow link shown to the user to consent.
2- Receiving the user response in a callback URL in your environment.
The call to this method creates a follow link ("follow" parameter) shown to the end user and a unique consent identifier ("consentId" parameter) that will allow you to keep track of the process. When the user opens the provided URL and finishes the process, you will receive in your system, through the callback URL indicated in the parameter yourConsentCallback, the token, unique identifier of this consent "consentId" and the globalPosition with all available accounts. The scheme sent to this callback URL is described in the consentGetCallback definition below.

parameters.name: Accept-version
parameters.description: API version requested in format: v.numberOfVersion

servicekey.description: License that identifies the client. If you don't have one, you can request it at https://www.arcopay.io.

service.description: Unique identifier for each bank. You can get the list of supported banks here /listOfSupportedBanks/.

grantType.description:
Type of requested consent. Accept one of the following:
- read: Account aggregation, you'll get the token to use with the method /transactions/
- payment: Payment initiation, you'll get the token to use it with the method /payments/

validUntil.description: Date until which the validity of the consent is requested in this format dd-mm-yyyy. The alowed range goes from the current date up to 90 days from the current date

dailyFrequency.description: This parametes is only applicable when the consent is required for account aggregation (grantType=read). It indicates the number of predicted daily updates of information. The allowed range of values goes from 1 to 4. The default value is 4.

yourConsentCallback.description: URL where you will receive the generated token and the global position of the available accounts. Must be HTTPS. See [/definitions/consentGetCallback](#/definitions/consentGetCallback)

urlRedirect.description: URL to which the user will be redirected once the consent obtaining process is finished. During the redirection, extra query parameters will be added, see [/definitions/urlRedirectConsent](#/definitions/urlRedirectConsent)

yourConsentCallbackError.description: URL where you will receive information in case of error. It must be HTTPS. See [/definitions/consentGetCallbackError](#/definitions/consentGetCallbackError)

200.description: Array of elements with consent information

parameters.name: Accept-version
parameters.description: API version requested in format: v.numberOfVersion

token.description: Token that you'll get through the /consent/get.

post.description: Extends the expiration time of an existing token to a maximum of 90 days from the current date.

parameters.name: Accept-version
parameters.description: API version requested in format: v.numberOfVersion

token.description: Token that you'll get through the method /consent/get.

validUntil.description: Date until which the renewal of consent is requested in dd-mm-yyyy format. The allowed range is from the current date, up to 90 days from the current date. The default value is 90 days from the current date.

urlRedirect.description: Only necessary if the renovation is for a consent obtained through the WidgetPSD2. URL to which the user will be redirected once the consent renew process is finished.

yourConsentCallback.description: Only necessary if the renovation is for a consent obtained through the WidgetPSD2. Must be HTTPS. See [/definitions/consentGetCallback](#/definitions/consentGetCallback)

yourConsentCallbackError.description: Only necessary if the renovation is for a consent obtained through the WidgetPSD2. URL where you will receive information in case of error. It must be HTTPS. See [/definitions/consentGetCallbackError](#/definitions/consentGetCallbackError)

post.description: It returns all available information about the consent,(token).

parameters.name: Accept-version
parameters.description: API version requested in format: v.numberOfVersion

token.description: Token that you'll get through the method /consent/get.

post.summary: Global position and transactions up to a given date.
post.description: This endpoint returns the list of the products and transactions for each of the products from the day in which they're been requested to the current date up to 2 years of transactions for these products, checking accounts and cards. The response header of each call to this endpoint will find a parameter called debug-id, which contains a unique identifier for each call, it'd be better to save it as the tech team will ask you to open support tickets.

parameters.name: Accept-version
parameters.description: API version requested in format: v.numberOfVersion

servicekey.description: License that identifies the client. If you don't have one you can request it at https://www.arcopay.io.

token.description: This token has been previously obtained following the PSD2 consent obtaining flow using the  method consent / get.

startDate.description: Date from which you want to get the transaction data in dd-mm-yyyy format. It’s mandatory when the value “products” differs from GLOBAL.

products.description: Bank product names (checking accounts or cards), separated by commas. It should be noted that in the first call to the API, we still do not know the names of the products that the user has contracted, so this parameter should receive the GLOBAL value.

200.description: Array with a list of banking products and transactions.

post.summary: Get the name of the bank linked to a specific IBAN.
post.description: Returns the name of the bank to which the provided IBAN belongs.

iban.description: IBAN of which you want to know the bank name to which it belongs. The accepted format is "XX0000000000000000000000". Being "XX" the ISO code of the country, followed by 2 control digits and another 20 digits that represent the basic code of the bank account.

200.description: Service to which an IBAN belongs. The "services" field is an array of objects, one object for each service to which the IBAN can belong.

get.summary: Categories list. Only for those servicekey that include this option.
get.description: Returns a list with all the categories retrieved by Arcopay own categorizer.

parameters.name: Accept-version
parameters.description: API version requested in format: v.numberOfVersion

parameters.name: countryCode
parameters.description: Country code, format ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_3166-1_alpha-2). For example ES for Spain. If no code is given, the full list is returned.

200.description: Array with the list of categories provided by Arcopay.

post.description: This method, although described in this documentation, is used exclusively by the Arcopay - Pay with your bank widget. The only exception is to have a contractual agreement allowing its use. The reason is that each PSD2 payment involves a control in terms of Prevention of Money Laundering and Terrorist Financing, which is already integrated in the widget. Initiate a payment: normal, instant, recurring. As well as in the method consent/get/, user interaction is required to process your payment. The call to this method responds to a follow link (“follow” parameter) shown to the end user together with a unique payment identifier ("paymentId" parameter) that will allow you to keep track of the process. When the user opens the provided URL and finishes the process, they will receive in their system, through the callback URL indicated in the parameter yourPaymentCallback, the token together with the unique identifier of this payment "paymentId". The scheme that is sent to this callback URL is described in the paymentInitiateCallback definition below.

parameters.name: Accept-version
parameters.description: API version requested in format: v.numberOfVersion

token.description: This token has been previously obtained following the PSD2 consent obtaining flow using the  method consent / get.

paymentType.description:
Payment type:
- normal -> regular bank transfer.
- instant -> instant bank transfer. Maximum allowed amount 15.000€ (150.000€ after July 2020). We discourage this option, as several banks charge very high fees to customers, and their APIS only inform the TPP of the fee once the customer has completed or canceled the payment, instead of informing previously to allow the TPP to select the type of payment based on commission.
- normalPeriodic -> normal transfer executed frequently, defined in the parameters firstQuotaDate, frequency and numberOfQuotas.
- instantPeriodic -> instant transfer executed regularly, defined in the parameters firstQuotaDate, frequency and numberOfQuotas.
- international -> normal transfer where the IBAN of the end user is international (outside Europe).

amount.description: Payment amount using the decimal point. Maximum 2 decimal places.

sourceIBAN.description: Source IBAN, with which the custommer wants to make the payment.

destinationCreditorName.description: Name of the destination account holder.

paymentDescription.description: Description or payment concept.  Maximum 140 characters.

yourPaymentCallback.description: URL where you will receive the token. Must be HTTPS.

firstQuotaDate.description: Mandatory for recurring payments only (installment payments), that is, when paymentType is normalPeriodic or instantPeriodic. Date in dd-mm-yyyy format.

frequency.description: Mandatory for recurring payments only (installment payments), that is, when paymentType is normalPeriodic or instantPeriodic. Allowed values: daily, weekly, everyTwoWeeks, monthly, everyTwoMonths, quarterly, semiAnnual, annual

numberOfQuotas.description: Mandatory for recurring payments only (installment payments), that is, when paymentType is normalPeriodic or instantPeriodic. Number of times the payment order will be executed. For example, if the order is made for returning a loan in 60 installments. The value will be 60.

urlRedirect.description: URL to which the user will be redirected once the payment is confirmed. During the redirection, extra query parameters will be added, see [/definitions/urlRedirectPayment](#/definitions/urlRedirectPayment)

yourPaymentCallbackError.description: URL where you will receive information in case of error. It must be HTTPS. See [/definitions/paymentInitiateCallbackError](#/definitions/paymentInitiateCallbackError)

200.description: Array of elements containing payment information.

parameters.name: Accept-version
parameters.description: API version requested in format: v.numberOfVersion

paymentId.description: The id that identifies the payment, obtained in payment/initiate.

extendedInformation.description: Additional payment information besides the status

post.description: Cancel the payment request, if it's possible.

parameters.name: Accept-version
parameters.description: API version requested in format: v.numberOfVersion

post.summary: Reverses a payment made with initiate
post.description: Reverses a previous payment. You will need the unique identifier of the initial payment "paymentId" to find the payment information.

parameters.name: Accept-version
parameters.description: API version requested in format: v.numberOfVersion

sourceIBAN.description: Source IBAN, with which the custommer wants to revert the payment. If not informed, the destination IBAN of the initial operation will be used.

200.description: Array of items containing reversed payment information.

parameters.name: Accept-version
parameters.description: API version requested in format: v.numberOfVersion

paymentRevertedId.description: The id that identifies the reversed payment, obtained in payment/revert

post.description: We offer three integrated ways for Payment Initiation. - The first is through calls to the methods described above. This way allows you to create a fully integrated user experience in your platform since all the UX design falls on you. - The second is the use of a javascript widget, which generates an iframe that can be integrated into any landing page. The technical details can be found in the document "Integration guide for Javascript Widget" that we have provided in the welcome email if this is the chosen option. - Finally, the one described in this method, which consists of a payment link with the parameterized values. This method receives the necessary data to make the payment and generates a one-time use SmartLink parameterized to provide it to the user to make a payment. Optionally, this same method allows sending the SmartLink by SMS. Before you can use this method, you must have prepared your environment to receive the completed payment response by following the "Integration guide for SmartLink Payments".

servicekey.description: License that identifies the client. If you don´t have one, you can request it at  https://www.arcopay.io.

JSONParameters.description: Parameters to configure the payment link options, such as amount, concept, and destination account, as indicated in the document "Integration guide for SmartLink Payments".

useLimit.description: Times the payment link can be used. For example, to collect a purchase, the value will be 1, and to collect a donation it may be greater than 1.

phoneNumber.description: Phone number with country code without spaces. For example, for Spain +34644501035. If no value is indicated, no message will be sent. You will only get the payment link in the reply.

bodyText.description: Text with a maximum of 80 characters, to which the SmartLink will be added. In case it is empty, by default "Use this link to continue with your payment of "import" at "merchant".

singleTab.description: Field to specify if payment is required without opening a separate tab for the bank login, i.e. by a direct redirection from the same window where the generated payment link is opened. Accepts values 1 or 0. It is mandatory to specify the creditor name in "sourceCreditorName" within the "JSONParameters" listing. It is also mandatory to specify a callback URL in the "urlCallbackSingleTab" field and "urlCallbackErrorSingleTab" if "singleTab" is set to 1.

urlCallbackSingleTab.description: Callback URL to be notified when the payment is successfully executed. Mandatory and only relevant if the "singleTab" parameter is set to 1.

urlCallbackErrorSingleTab.description: Callback URL to be notified when there is an error in the payment. Mandatory and only relevant if the "singleTab" parameter is set to 1.

200.description: Array with the URL to the landing page with the payment information and, if SMS delivery has been requested, information on the status of the delivery.

post.description: Initiates a normal, instant o periodic payment. The main difference between this endpoint and / payment / initiate / is that in this case minimal interaction is required from the customer to process the payment. The call to this method responds with a follow link type ("follow" parameter) and a unique identifier of the payment ("paymentId" parameter) that will allow you to trace the process. When the user opens the provided URL, a Money Laundering Prevention process will be carried out and, if necessary, an extra check will be carried out prior to the start of payment. Once the process is finished, you will receive in your system, through the callback URL indicated in the yourPaymentCallback parameter, the unique identifier of this payment "paymentId"

parameters.name: Accept-version
parameters.description: API version requested in format: v.numberOfVersion

service.description: Unique identifier for each bank. You can get the list of supported banks here /listOfSupportedBanks/.

ownerName.description: Owner name of the account to begin with Money Laundering Prevention process.

paymentType.description:
Payment type:
- normal -> regular bank transfer.
- instant -> instant bank transfer. Maximum allowed amount 15.000€ (150.000€ after July 2020). We discourage this option, as several banks charge very high fees to customers, and their APIS only inform the TPP of the fee once the customer has completed or canceled the payment, instead of informing previously to allow the TPP to select the type of payment based on commission.
- normalPeriodic -> normal transfer executed frequently, defined in the parameters firstQuotaDate, frequency and numberOfQuotas.
- instantPeriodic -> instant transfer executed regularly, defined in the parameters firstQuotaDate, frequency and numberOfQuotas.
- international -> normal transfer where the IBAN of the end user is international (outside Europe).

amount.description: Payment amount using the decimal point. Maximum 2 decimal places.

sourceIBAN.description: Source IBAN, with which the custommer wants to make the payment. If it's not provided, the custommer will be able to select the IBAN on the bank interface.

destinationCreditorName.description: Name of the destination account holder.

paymentDescription.description: Description or payment concept.  Maximum 140 characters.

yourPaymentCallback.description: URL where you will receive the token. Must be HTTPS.

firstQuotaDate.description: Mandatory for recurring payments only (installment payments), that is, when paymentType is normalPeriodic or instantPeriodic. Date in dd-mm-yyyy format.

frequency.description: Mandatory for recurring payments only (installment payments), that is, when paymentType is normalPeriodic or instantPeriodic. Allowed values: daily, weekly, everyTwoWeeks, monthly, everyTwoMonths, quarterly, semiAnnual, annual

numberOfQuotas.description: Mandatory for recurring payments only (installment payments), that is, when paymentType is normalPeriodic or instantPeriodic. Number of times the payment order will be executed. For example, if the order is made for returning a loan in 60 installments. The value will be 60.

urlRedirect.description: URL to which the user will be redirected once the payment is confirmed. During the redirection, extra query parameters will be added, see [/definitions/urlRedirectPayment](#/definitions/urlRedirectPayment)

yourPaymentCallbackError.description: URL where you will receive information in case of error. It must be HTTPS. See [/definitions/paymentInitiateCallbackError](#/definitions/paymentInitiateCallbackError)

200.description: Array of elements containing payment information.

post.description: Initiates a bulk payment. The call to this method responds with a follow link type ("follow" parameter) and a unique identifier of the payment ("paymentId" parameter) that will allow you to trace the process. When the user opens the provided URL, a Money Laundering Prevention process will be carried out and, if necessary, an extra check will be carried out prior to the start of payment. Once the process is finished, you will receive in your system, through the callback URL indicated in the yourPaymentCallback parameter, the unique identifier of this payment "paymentId"

parameters.name: Accept-version
parameters.description: API version requested in format: v.numberOfVersion

service.description: Unique identifier for each bank. You can get the list of supported banks here /listOfSupportedBanks/.

ownerName.description: Owner name of the account to begin with Money Laundering Prevention process.

sourceIBAN.description: Source IBAN, with which the custommer wants to make the payment.

payments.description: JSON that contains as many [/definitions/bulkPaymentItem](#/definitions/bulkPaymentItem) elements as there are payments to be made.

urlRedirect.description: URL to which the user will be redirected once the payment is confirmed. During the redirection, extra query parameters will be added, see [/definitions/urlRedirectPayment](#/definitions/urlRedirectPayment)

yourPaymentCallback.description: URL where you will receive the token. Must be HTTPS.

yourPaymentCallbackError.description: URL where you will receive information in case of error. It must be HTTPS. See [/definitions/paymentInitiateCallbackError](#/definitions/paymentInitiateCallbackError)

200.description: Array of elements containing payment information.

post.description: Returns information about the client's license and API usage.

parameters.name: Accept-version
parameters.description: API version requested in format: v.numberOfVersion

details.description: It indicates the request of knowing extra details. The default value is 0

timePeriod.description: Check date filter. Only applies if details = 1. Possible values: 24h or 30d

consentId.description: String of 36 characters with uuid format 83c5036d-b7ce-f87e-99ef-7b966f16d9f5 that identifies the consent operation.

post.summary: Si dispone de un token de refresco, puede refrescarlo con este método. Este token estará solo disponible dependiendo del caso de uso de algunos clientes.
post.description: Dado un token de refresco, realiza el refresco de dicho token e informa el token resultante.

service.description: Unique identifier for each bank. You can get the list of supported banks here /listOfSupportedBanks/.

post.description: This endpoint collects a set of credentials for batch processing.

requestBody.description: Request body with servicekey information and an array of credentials to be processed.

post.description: Obtains the results of the requested batch processing.

batchId.description: Identifier of the previously sent batch from which the result is to be obtained.

consentId.description: String of 36 characters with uuid format 83c5036d-b7ce-f87e-99ef-7b966f16d9f5 that identifies the consent operation.

token.description: This token has been previously obtained following the PSD2 consent obtaining flow using the method consent/get.

refreshToken.description: Value of the refresh token after the refresh has been performed.

expiresIn.description: Seconds during which the access token will remain valid.

counter.description: Calls made during the current billing cycle.

remaining_calls.description: Remaining calls for the current billing cycle.

date_renewal.description: Call counter renewal date, in dd-mm-yyyy format.

product.description: Unique identifier for a product: IBAN or credit card numbers.

type.description: Product type: "checking" for checking account or "card" for credit card. It is only possible to retrieve information on these two types of products through the PSD2 channel, in contrast to non-PSD2 aggregation, where it is also possible to retrieve information on loans, credit lines, insurance and investment products.

balance.description: Available balance of the product. This balance will always exist, the rest of balance values may or may not exist.

description.description: Product name. Sometimes it is an alias chosen by the user or it can match the product value.

iban.description: The IBAN of the account. Available for checking and saving products.

pan.description: The PAN of the account. Available for card and debit products.

is_owner.description: Indicates if the user that is giving the consent is the owner of the products (in some cases, the bank is informing with the name of the connected user, and not the owner of the product). 0 -> Not a holder. 1-> Is the account holder.

holders.description: Attribute only available when the product is of type checking.

items.description: Array of holders and authorized of each product.

role.description: Role of the person. Owner | Authorized.

date.description: Transaction date in the following format dd-mm-yyyy.

date2.description: Value date in the following format dd-mm-yyyy.

balance.description: Product balance right after the transaction has been made.

categoryId.description: Category id. Only for those servicekeys that include this option. Categories list: https://apipsd2.arcopay.io/transactions/categories/?countryCode=ES.

transactionId.description: Unique identifier for that 32-character transaction product. VERY IMPORTANT: Do not use it as a unique index on a table of all customer transactions, there may be collisions, since only ensures that a single value per transaction and product. Yes, it would be valid to use two columns that include a product id and this transactionId as a unique index in a transaction table.

mcc.description: Merchant Category Code as it is defined by ISO 18245. The value will be 0 if MCC is not available.

creditorName.description: The holder of the transaction. The value will be empty if no holder is available.

debtorName.description: The payee of the transaction. The value will be empty if no payee is available.

countryCode.description: Country code in this format ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_3166-1_alpha-2).

service.description: Unique identifier for the bank in our API.

paymentsSupported.description: Values 0 or 1, depending on whether it supports Payment Initiation (método /payments/)

swift.description: Código SWIFT in this format ISO 9362 (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_9362).

image.description: Bank logo URL in transparent PNG format of 128x128 pixels.

imageSVG.description: Bank logo URL in SVG format.

extendedInformation.description: Only if showExtendedInformation parameter is set to 1

normalPayment.description: 1 if normal payment PSD2 is supported. 0 if it is not supported.

instantPayment.description: 1 if instant payment PSD2 is supported. 0 if it is not supported.

periodicPayment.description: 1 if it supports PSD2 periodic payment. 0 if it does not.

instantPeriodicPayment.description: 1 if it supports instant payment in PSD2 periodic mode. 0 if it does not.

svaPayment.description: 1 if it supports SVA payment PSD2. 0 if it does not.

bulkPayment.description: 1 if bulk payment PSD2 is supported. 0 if it is not supported.

follow.description: Follow link to authorize the payment. The URL can be larger than 255 characters.

paymentId.description: String of 36 characters with uuid format 83c5036d-b7ce-f87e-99ef-7b966f16d9f5 that identifies the payment transaction.

status.description:
Status of the payment. Can be
  - OK -> The payment has been made correctly.
  - KO -> Payment has not been made.
  - RJCT -> Payment has been rejected.
  - CNCL -> Payment has been cancelled.

paymentType.description: Payment type. Only if extendedInformation is set to 1.

currency.description: Currency code in format [ISO 4217](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_4217). Only if extendedInformation is set to 1.

amount.description: Payment amount. Only if extendedInformation is set to 1.

sourceIBAN.description: Origin IBAN. Only if extendedInformation is set to 1.

destinationIBAN.description: Destination IBAN. Only if extendedInformation is set to 1.

destinationCreditorName.description: Recipient name. Only if extendedInformation is set to 1.

paymentDescription.description: Payment description. Only if extendedInformation is set to 1.

ownerName.description: Origin account owner name. Only if extendedInformation is set to 1.

fecha.description: Date in which the payment is made. Only if extendedInformation is set to 1.

status.description: If the revert process has been executed successfully the status will be ACCP.

Status of the reverted payment. Can be
  - RCVD -> initial order has been received.
  - PDNG -> processed order and send by EDItran.
  - RJCT -> the destination EDItran rejected the initiation of the payment for the reversal.
  - ACCP -> the destination EDItran accepted the initiation of the payment for the reversal.
If the cancellation process has been executed successfully the status will be CANC.

follow.description: Sometimes there will be required a confirmation from the user in order to cancel the payment, in that cases, this parameter will be informed with a link, otherwise it will be empty.
URL shown to the user in order to accept the consent request. The URL can be larger than 255 characters.

consentId.description: String of 36 characters with uuid format 83c5036d-b7ce-f87e-99ef-7b966f16d9f5 that identifies the consent operation.

consentGetCallback.description: This definition describes the scheme used by Aftebanks to send to the callback URL specified by the client in the yourConsentCallback parameter of the /consent/get/ method

token.description: Token to be used in those methods that require tokens as parameters /payments/ initiate and /transactions.

globalPosition.description: List of all the available products. See [/definitions/transactions](#/definitions/transactions)

paymentInitiateCallback.description: This definition describes the scheme used by Aftebanks to send to the callback URL specified by the client in the yourConsentCallback parameter of the /consent/get/ method

token.description: Token to be used in methods that require tokens as parameters /payments/ initiate and /transactions.

consentGetCallbackError.description: This definition describes the scheme used by Arcopay to send to the error callback URL specified by the client in the yourConsentCallbackError parameter of the /consent/get method

paymentInitiateCallbackError.description: This definition describes the scheme used by Arcopay to send to the error callback URL specified by the customer in the yourPaymentCallbackError parameter of the /payment/initiate method.

linkForPayment.description: URL with the UX to pay. Example: https://www.arcopay.io/payWithYourBank/cEv-DOVDMro/

code.description: Possible values: 0, OK; 1, Shipping error; 2, Shipping not requested

phoneNumber.description: The phone number to which the message has been sent.

grantType.description: Reports the type of deleted token. Possible values: payment or read.

message.description: Message, in the language of the bank indicating the successful cancellation of the token

follow.description: URL shown to the user in order to complete the renewal process. The URL can be larger than 255 characters.

consentId.description: String of 36 characters with uuid format 83c5036d-b7ce-f87e-99ef-7b966f16d9f5 that identifies the consent operation.

ibanToService.description: Service to which an IBAN belongs. The "services" field is an array of objects, one object for each service to which the IBAN can belong.

items.description: Service to which an IBAN belongs.

service.description: Service to which an IBAN belongs.

grantType.description: Type of requested consent: read or payment

creationDate.description: Date on which the consent was established in format dd-mm-yyyy.

validUntil.description: Expiration date of consent in dd-mm-yyyy format.

timestamp.description: Date of access in timestamp format.

currency.description: Only if grantType=payment. Currency in this format [ISO 4217](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_4217).

amount.description: Only if grantType=payment. Amount of the transaction, if it is a Payment Initiation operation.

destinationIBAN.description: Only if grantType=payment. Bank account number of origin.

numberOfTransactionsCollected.description: Only if grantType=read. Bank account number of origin.

message.description: Detailed description of the found error, in the bank language.

code.description:
Feasible values
  - 1 -> Generic.
  - 50 -> Incorrect parameter in API call.
  - C000 -> Generic error with consent.
  - C001 -> Consent invalid.
  - C002 -> Consent process was not finished.
  - C003 -> Consent was not created for the requested product.
  - C004 -> Consent expired.
  - C005 -> Consent daily limit reached.
  - T000 -> Generic error with transactions.
  - T001 -> Consent was not created for the requested product.
  - P000 -> Generic error with payment initiation.
  - P001 -> Invalid sourceIBAN.
  - P002 -> Invalid format of destinationIBAN.
  - P003 -> Invalid format of sourceIBAN.
  - P004 -> There are no sufficient funds available in debtor account.
  - P005 -> Payment amount exceeds account limit.
  - B000 -> Incorrect value in one of the batch parameters.

id.description: Numerical identifier associated with the category

color.description: Suggested color to display with the category

icon.description: Suggested icon according to the icon collection maintained by Google https://material.io/resources/icons/

urlRedirectConsent.description: Extra information about query parameters added to urlRedirect after consent/get/:

statusAB.description:
Information extra about a possible error or result of communication with the callback
  - 200 -> response from the callback was a valid JSON with result = OK.
  - 403 -> response from the callback was an invalid JSON.
  - 402 -> response from the callback was a valid JSON, but with unexpected structure.
  - 401 -> response from the callback was a valid JSON with result = KO
  - 404 -> wrong credentials
  - 400 -> generic error

consentStatusAB.description: Current value of the consent status.

urlRedirectPayment.description: Extra information about query parameters added to urlRedirect after payment/initiate/:

statusAB.description:
Information extra about a possible error or result of communication with the callback
  - 200 -> response from the callback was a valid JSON with result = OK.
  - 403 -> response from the callback was an invalid JSON.
  - 402 -> response from the callback was a valid JSON, but with unexpected structure.
  - 401 -> response from the callback was a valid JSON with result = KO
  - 404 -> wrong credentials
  - 400 -> generic error

paymentStatusAB.description: Current value of the payment status.

amount.description: Payment amount using the decimal point. Maximum 2 decimal places.

destinationCreditorName.description: Name of the destination account holder.

paymentDescription.description: Description or payment concept.  Maximum 140 characters.

debugID.description: Debug-id, identifier of the request to obtain results.

startTimeStamp.description: Date and time of start of processing.

endTimeStamp.description: Date and time of end of processing.

totalProductsProcessed.description: Products obtained by processing the credentials.

status.description: Message informing about the processing status of the batch. Possible values: inProgress|finished|failed|cancelled

credentialId.description: Unique identifier per credential indicated by the customer.

result.description: It shall contain the response pattern of /transactions/ or Error, depending on the result of the execution.

credentialId.description: Unique numeric identifier per credential indicated by the client. When collecting the information, an array will be obtained where each credentialId will return the information of its associated credential.

service.description: Unique identifier for each bank. The list of supported banks is obtained in /listOfSupportedBanks/. Mandatory if no value is reported in the token parameter.

token.description: Token that has been obtained through the /consent/get method.

products.description: Names of bank products (current accounts, cards, etc), separated by commas.

startDate.description: Date from which we want to obtain the movements in dd-mm-yyyy format.

accessToken.description: Access token of the bank. Mandatory if no value is reported in the token parameter. This token will only be available depending on the use case of some clients.

consentId.description: Bank consent id. Mandatory if no value is reported in the token parameter. This consentId will only be available depending on the use case of some clients.
